subject: Human Resources
title: At-Will Employment
description: A quick, practical guide to At-Will Employment.

hook.text: In 49 U.S. states, your employer can fire you tomorrow for no reason at all. It's called at-will employment.

buildup.text: At-will means either party—employer or employee—can end the relationship at any time, without cause or notice.

discovery.text: The doctrine dates to 1877. Most other developed countries require cause for dismissal—the U.S. is an outlier.

twist.text: At-will isn't absolute. Exceptions include discrimination, retaliation, whistleblowing, and implied contracts.

climax.text: Understanding at-will—and its limits—is the foundation of every employment law decision HR professionals make.

punchline.text: At-will gives freedom. Its exceptions give protection.

quiz.question: What does at-will employment mean?
options: Either party can end employment at any time without cause
